display.purpose: Stat and hero numbers in summary tiles. Always tabular-nums.

title.purpose: Dialog titles, card titles, section headers.

body.purpose: Default text, set on body and inherited everywhere. 14px / 500.

label.purpose: Field labels above inputs, meta, captions.

eyebrow.purpose: Sidebar section headers only — uppercase, 0.06em tracking, low opacity.

shadows.name: no-shadow
shadows.purpose: Default. Depth is built from white overlays and hairline borders, never box-shadow.

shadows.name: overlay-lift
shadows.purpose: The only elevation mechanism — a lighter white overlay, not a shadow.

shadows.name: network-backdrop
shadows.purpose: Exception, not a pattern: the full-screen network-loss overlay only.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Global transition on background-color, border-color, color, opacity for all interactive elements.

motion.name: sidebar-collapse
motion.purpose: Sidebar width and nav-item padding/opacity transitions.

zIndex.name: z-overlay
zIndex.purpose: Dropdowns, popovers, dialogs — matches Tailwind z-50.

zIndex.name: z-banner
zIndex.purpose: Persistent app banners (update-download / update-available), above the overlays.

zIndex.name: z-toast
zIndex.purpose: Transient toasts — the top layer, above the banner.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one Action Blue voice — primary actions only.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #3b82f6; color: #fff; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 120ms ease-out, opacity 120ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #2563eb; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #60a5fa; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Icon/text actions in dense tables and cards.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #9ca3af; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: color 120ms ease-out, background-color 120ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { color: #d4d4d8; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.055); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #52525b; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: The shared inputClass — dark field, hairline border, border-shift focus.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; background: #27272a; border: 1px solid #3f3f46; border-radius: 10px; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 14px; color: #fff; transition: border-color 120ms ease-out, background-color 120ms ease-out; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #71717a; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #71717a; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); } .ds-input.error { border-color: #ef4444; }

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: Tinted state chip — foreground -400, fill /10. Import color from campaignTracking.ts.
components.css: .ds-status-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 2px 8px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; color: #60a5fa; background: rgba(59,130,246,0.10); }

components.name: Status Dot
components.description: Compact status indicator for dense lists and kanban rows.
components.css: .ds-status-dot { display: inline-block; width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #60a5fa; }

components.name: Summary Widget Card
components.description: Compact dashboard tile: label + big tabular metric. Tightened padding.
components.css: .ds-widget { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; background: #171717; border: 1px solid #2a2a2a; border-radius: 14px; padding: 16px; } .ds-widget-label { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; color: #9ca3af; } .ds-widget-metric { font-size: 24px; font-weight: 600; color: #fff; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

components.name: Kanban Row
components.description: The dense, monospaced, tappable leaf row — the signature line of the system.
components.css: .ds-kanban-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; width: 100%; text-align: left; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.04); border: none; border-left: 2px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.14); border-radius: 8px; padding: 6px 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: filter 120ms ease-out, transform 120ms ease-out; } .ds-kanban-row:hover { filter: brightness(1.1); } .ds-kanban-row:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-kanban-code { font-family: 'Google Sans', monospace; font-size: 12px; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: #fff; } .ds-kanban-amount { margin-left: auto; font-size: 12px; color: #9ca3af; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-kanban-row { transition: none; } .ds-kanban-row:active { transform: none; } }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: The 260px navigation rail item — hover/active use white overlays.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 4px 8px; margin: 0 6px; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: #fff; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 120ms ease-out; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.055); } .ds-nav-item.active { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); font-weight: 600; } .ds-nav-icon { width: 18px; height: 18px; flex-shrink: 0; opacity: 0.65; border-radius: 4px; background: currentColor; } .ds-nav-item.active .ds-nav-icon { opacity: 1; }

narrative.overview: Bluu Rock MGMT is a dark, dense operations console for an internal team — one they live inside for eight hours a day. The design philosophy is total deference: chrome recedes to near-black, type is small, spacing is tight, and the data is the interface. It borrows its posture from Notion and Linear — an instrument panel, not a marketing site. Color is rationed like a signal, never spent as decoration: the interface is greyscale by default and reaches for hue only when hue means a status, a priority, or a category. Depth is built from layered translucent-white overlays on a dark ground, not from drop shadows. Motion is fast and almost subliminal: 120ms ease-out on color and opacity, a small scale/brightness nudge on press. This system explicitly rejects the SaaS marketing look — no gradient heroes, no glassmorphism, no oversized display type, no color used for mood.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black canvas (#0A0A0A), low-chroma surfaces, 14px base type, tight spacing.
Greyscale by default; color is strictly semantic (status / priority / category).
Soft elevation from translucent white overlays + hairline borders — never shadows.
Every number is tabular-nums; every code/ID is font-mono.
Fast, subtle motion: 120ms ease-out; hover:brightness-110 active:scale-[0.98] on tappable rows.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Action Blue is the only non-semantic color on any screen, and it marks the primary action or current selection — nothing else. Its scarcity is what makes it read.

rules.name: The Semantic-Only Rule
rules.body: Color is forbidden as decoration. If a green, orange, or red pixel does not encode a status, priority, or category, remove it. Pull every state color from campaignTracking.ts; never hardcode a themeable hex.

rules.name: The Overlay-Not-Grey Rule
rules.body: Interior surfaces are translucent white on the dark ground, not solid greys — this is what gives the soft, layered depth.

rules.name: The Tabular Rule
rules.body: Every count, amount, and metric uses tabular-nums; every code or ID (CR0001) uses font-mono text-xs. Numbers must align in columns — always.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Google Sans carries the entire UI. Never introduce a display or accent typeface; hierarchy comes from weight, size, and color, never from a second family.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Depth comes from a lighter overlay and a hairline border, never from a box-shadow. If a surface needs to lift, raise its white overlay opacity — do not add a shadow.

rules.name: The Hairline Rule
rules.body: Every border is a single hairline: #2a2a2a on opaque surfaces, rgba(255,255,255,0.07) on overlays. No heavy dividers, no double borders.

rules.name: The Named-Layer Rule
rules.body: Stacking order is expressed with the --z-* scale (--z-overlay < --z-banner < --z-toast), never a literal like z-[9999]. A raw magic z-index is drift — replace it with the layer whose name matches its role.

dos: Do keep the canvas near-black (#0A0A0A) and let chrome recede — the data is the interface.
Do build depth from the white-overlay recipe (rgba(255,255,255,0.025) bg / 0.07 border) plus hairline borders.
Do pull every status/priority color from campaignTracking.ts and apply the -400 text / /10 fill / /30 border triad.
Do put tabular-nums on every count, amount, and metric, and font-mono text-xs on every code/ID (CR0001).
Do use only src/components/ui components, only @tabler/icons-react / lucide-react icons, and only Avatar for images.
Do shape Skeletons to the final layout, write empty states as one quiet text-muted-foreground line, and toast every mutation outcome.
Do keep motion to 120ms transitions and hover:brightness-110 active:scale-[0.98] on tappable rows.
Do stack app surfaces with the --z-* scale (--z-overlay < --z-banner < --z-toast) via var(--z-*).
Do make it read like custom-requests/page.tsx. If it doesn't, reconcile.
donts: Don't add drop shadows for depth — raise the white overlay instead (The No-Shadow Rule).
Don't use color as decoration; if a colored pixel doesn't encode state, category, or the one Action Blue voice, remove it (The Semantic-Only Rule).
Don't hardcode a themeable hex — use the CSS variables / Tailwind tokens.
Don't introduce another component library, hand-roll a primitive that exists in src/components/ui, or use icons outside @tabler/icons-react / lucide-react.
Don't use a raw — always Avatar.
Don't reach for a modal first, drop a spinner into the middle of a layout, or ship an illustration empty state.
Don't introduce a second font family, oversized display type, gradient text/heros, or glassmorphism — this is not a marketing site.
Don't use a colored side-stripe as a decorative accent; the only left-border in the system is the kanban row's functional border-l-2 overlay edge.
Don't reach for a magic z-[9999]; use the semantic --z-* layer whose name matches the role (The Named-Layer Rule).
